title: Creamy Mushroom Spaghetti
tagline: Browned mushrooms in a garlicky parmesan cream sauce over spaghetti.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients. At ~820 kcal a serving this is a big meal — the cream and cheese carry most of the fat.
Cut it down: swap the heavy cream for whole milk + 1 tsp cornflour (thickens the same way) to save ~180 kcal per serving, and use 1 tbsp olive oil instead of 3.

Protein boost: add 100 g of soaked, squeezed soya chunks (50 g per serving) — chop them small, brown them with the mushrooms so they soak up flavour. Adds ~30 g protein across the pair and turns a carb-heavy plate into a proper gym meal. Grilled chicken (150 g) works too if you're not keeping it veg.

items: 100 g dry soya chunks, soaked in hot water and squeezed dry, finely chopped

steps.title: Boil the spaghetti
steps.body: Bring a large pot of water (4-5 litres) to a rolling boil, add 1-2 tbsp salt, then add the 225 g spaghetti. Note: get it to a full boil BEFORE adding the pasta — if you drop it in early, crank the heat to high and stir frequently so it doesn't clump or stick. Cook to al dente (usually 8-10 min), then drain. Reserve 1/2 cup of pasta water; don't rinse the spaghetti.

steps.title: Sweat the aromatics
steps.body: In a large skillet heat 2 tbsp olive oil + 1 tbsp (15 g) butter over medium. Add 2 minced garlic cloves and the chopped onion (~80 g) and cook 3-4 minutes until soft and translucent.

steps.title: Brown the mushrooms (don't rush)
steps.body: Add the 340 g sliced mushrooms (and the chopped 100 g soaked soya chunks now, if using). Cook on medium-high 7-10 minutes. They'll release water first — keep cooking until ALL that liquid evaporates and the mushrooms start to brown. This is the whole flavour base; if you add cream while they're still watery the sauce tastes flat.

steps.title: Make the cream sauce
steps.body: Lower the heat. Pour in 120 ml heavy cream and let it simmer gently 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened. Season with 1/2 tsp black pepper and salt to taste.

steps.title: Melt in the cheese
steps.body: Stir in 50 g grated parmesan until it melts into a smooth sauce. If it's too thick, loosen it with a splash of the reserved pasta water.

steps.title: Toss and serve
steps.body: Add the drained spaghetti to the skillet and toss 1-2 minutes so every strand is coated, adding more pasta water if needed for a glossy sauce. Plate and finish with 2 tbsp chopped parsley and extra parmesan.

notes: The single most important step is drying out the mushrooms fully before the cream goes in — it concentrates the flavour and gives a proper sauce texture.
A splash of dry white wine after the mushrooms brown (let it cook off 2-3 min) adds depth, but it's optional and not needed.
Scales cleanly to 4 — double everything and use ~1.5 lb mushrooms.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
